title: alnus rohlenae
intro: Alnus rohlenae, also known as the fossil alder, is an extinct species of tree that once existed during the Eocene epoch. Research suggests that Alnus rohlenae may have had medicinal properties similar to those of the black alder, with potential applications in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits associated with Alnus rohlenae, although largely theoretical, may include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, due to its extinction status, herbal preparations using this plant are not possible, and any potential uses are based on its similarity to other alder species.
